item.title: Europe’s Far Right Is Turning on Trump
item.description: The president’s attempt to influence elections across the Atlantic is backfiring.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Stanley-Becker

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: March Madness
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: Photos: Eid al-Fitr and the End of Ramadan 2026
item.description: Celebrations of the Muslim festival of Eid al-Fitr, which marks the end of the month of Ramadan, took place this weekend in countries around the world.
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: American Aviation Is Near Collapse
item.description: Fatal crashes, overstressed controllers, and endless security lines reveal a system teetering on the brink of failure.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: The Strategic Follies of the Islamic Republic
item.description: The past half century reveals a record not of constant brilliance, but of consistent folly.
item.dc:creator: Eliot A. Cohen

item.title: TheIn Living ColorEffect
item.description: How a radical sketch-comedy show helped usher in the cultural boom of Black comedy in the 1990s
item.dc:creator: Geoff Bennett

item.title: The Strangeness of a Trumpified Capital
item.description: The festooning of the president’s name and likeness across Washington, D.C., is consistent with authoritarian tendencies.
item.dc:creator: Gal Beckerman

item.title: ‘Intensely Southern and Only Faintly Jewish’
item.description: Nicholas Lemann recalls an unusual and sometimes unsettling family history—his own.
item.dc:creator: Adam Kirsch

item.title: Why I Stopped Forcing My Kid to Share
item.dc:creator: Jenisha Watts

item.title: Chuck Norris Played Too Good a Bad Guy to Stay One
item.description: Before he became an avatar of manly hyperbole, he held his own against Bruce Lee.
item.dc:creator: Chris Klimek

item.title: How Trump Killed Conservatism
item.description: The president has cultivated and encouraged the ugliest passions within the GOP, dousing the embers of hate with kerosene.
item.dc:creator: Peter Wehner

item.title: How the Midwest Became the Place to Move
item.dc:creator: Olga Khazan

item.title: Canceled by Hinge
item.description: People are being banned from dating apps without knowing why.
item.dc:creator: Annie Joy Williams

item.title: Trump’s Eye Is Already on Cuba
item.description: “Regime change is lined up,” awaiting the president’s signal, according to one administration official.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Salama

item.title: A Friend Gifts Me a Paper Bag of Honeycomb
item.dc:creator: Nina C. Peláez

item.title: The Death of Millennial Feminism
item.description: Lindy West has unwittingly written the obituary for an era.
item.dc:creator: Helen Lewis

item.title: The Provocation ofThe Pitt
item.description: Plus: four new books to look out for, a children’s show for adults, and other culture and entertainment recommendations
item.dc:creator: Josh Tyrangiel

item.title: The Lecture I Couldn’t Give
item.description: Now more than ever, our armed forces need to understand the history of civil-military relations.
item.dc:creator: Kori Schake

item.title: How to Fix DHS
item.description: The answer to the agency’s abuses is not reform; it is wholesale disassembly and restructuring.
item.dc:creator: Paul Rosenzweig

item.title: The First AI Crisis Is Psychological
item.description: The economic shocks may well be coming, but we have already entered an age of profound uncertainty about ourselves and the world around us.
item.dc:creator: Nick Dothée
